title: dicksonia squarrosa var. gracilis
intro: Dicksonia squarrosa var. gracilis, also known as the Rough Tree Fern, is a variety of tree fern native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its dark green, lance-shaped fronds. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Dicksonia squarrosa var. gracilis include reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and boosted immunity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
